typecheck.command: xcodebuild build-for-testing -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS,arch=arm64" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-typecheck CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

blockingIssues.description: Added backfill runtime test is source-string scanning in BackfillSchedulerTests and does not execute UsageAggregator.refreshAll/runScheduledBackfillIfNeeded coexistence behavior; required runtime integration coverage remains unmet.
BackfillCursorStore monotonic guard now allows tiny backward movement via >= -monotonicEpsilon, reopening negative epsilon allowance contrary to strict no-backward requirement.

Fix commit does not modify UsageAggregator runtime wiring or runtime integration tests tied to the original failed feature scope, so prior runtime-path validation gap is not directly remediated by this feature.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require explicit command-level evidence of mandated startup context reads before allowing skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true, and add scrutiny checklist enforcement.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both rerun fix reviews reported handoffs claiming followedProcedure=true while commandsRun omitted mission.md/AGENTS.md/.factory/library read evidence.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add mission-start guidance that mission-scoped docs are authoritative when shared library docs are stale/misaligned, and require workers to call out detected drift in handoffs.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both fix reviews flagged .factory/library architecture/user-testing references as switcher-centric and misaligned with token-accounting/backfill mission context.

rejectedObservations.observation: Duplicate shared-state reports that .factory/library architecture/user-testing content remains switcher-centric.
